sourceMovie.title: Money Talks
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Money Talks (1997) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hot Fuzz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Money Talks clicked for you, Hot Fuzz (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Man on a Ledge
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Money Talks clicked for you, Man on a Ledge (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Money Talks?
faq.answer: Try Hot Fuzz, Man on a Ledge, Faster—each shares DNA with Money Talks while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Money Talks?
faq.answer: Money Talks (1997) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Money Talks worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
